name: logs
context: View, follow, filter, and manage Docker container log output from stdout and stderr
info: This sub-skill covers docker logs for viewing container output. Docker captures everything written to stdout and stderr by the container's main process and stores it via the configured logging driver. The default json-file driver writes logs to JSON files on the host. Commands include following live output (-f), showing timestamps (--since, --until), limiting output (--tail), and filtering by time range. Logging drivers determine where logs are stored: json-file (default, local), journald (systemd), syslog (remote syslog), fluentd (log aggregator), awslogs (CloudWatch), gcplogs (Cloud Logging), and none (discard). Configure log rotation to prevent disk exhaustion. In production, forward logs to a centralized system (ELK, Loki, Datadog) via the appropriate logging driver or a sidecar container. This sub-skill covers log access and configuration; see [[docker/containers/exec]] for interactive debugging.
triggers: set up log rotation for docker
body:
# Docker Container Logs

Docker captures everything written to stdout and stderr by a container's main process (PID 1). The `docker logs` command retrieves this output. The logging driver determines how and where logs are stored.

## Basic Usage

```bash
# View all logs
docker logs myapp

# Follow (stream) live output
docker logs -f myapp

# Show last N lines
docker logs --tail 100 myapp

# Follow starting from last 50 lines
docker logs -f --tail 50 myapp

# Show timestamps
docker logs -t myapp

# Separate stdout and stderr
docker logs myapp 2>/dev/null     # stdout only
docker logs myapp 1>/dev/null     # stderr only
```

## Time-Based Filtering

Filter logs by time range to find specific events:

```bash
# Logs since a specific time
docker logs --since 2026-04-10T10:00:00 myapp

# Logs from the last 30 minutes
docker logs --since 30m myapp

# Logs from the last 2 hours
docker logs --since 2h myapp

# Logs between two times
docker logs --since 2026-04-10T08:00:00 --until 2026-04-10T09:00:00 myapp

# Combine with tail for recent errors
docker logs --since 10m --tail 50 myapp
```

## Compose Logs

Docker Compose provides aggregated logs across all services:

```bash
# All services
docker compose logs

# Follow all services
docker compose logs -f

# Specific service(s)
docker compose logs -f web api

# Last 100 lines per service
docker compose logs --tail 100

# Since a time
docker compose logs --since 30m web

# No color (for piping/parsing)
docker compose logs --no-color web | grep ERROR
```

See [[docker/compose]] for multi-service orchestration.

## Logging Drivers

The logging driver determines where container logs are stored:

| Driver | Destination | docker logs? | Use Case |
|---|---|---|---|
| `json-file` | Local JSON files | Yes | Default, development, small deployments |
| `local` | Local optimized format | Yes | Better performance than json-file |
| `journald` | systemd journal | Yes | Linux servers with systemd |
| `syslog` | Remote syslog server | No | Traditional log aggregation |
| `fluentd` | Fluentd collector | No | Flexible log routing and processing |
| `awslogs` | AWS CloudWatch Logs | No | AWS deployments |
| `gcplogs` | Google Cloud Logging | No | GCP deployments |
| `none` | Discarded | No | High-throughput apps logging internally |

### Setting the Logging Driver

```bash
# Per container
docker run -d --log-driver json-file --log-opt max-size=10m --log-opt max-file=3 myapp

# Per container (journald)
docker run -d --log-driver journald myapp

# Per container (awslogs)
docker run -d \
  --log-driver awslogs \
  --log-opt awslogs-region=us-east-1 \
  --log-opt awslogs-group=/ecs/myapp \
  --log-opt awslogs-stream-prefix=web \
  myapp
```

### Default Logging Driver

Configure the daemon-wide default in `/etc/docker/daemon.json`:

```json
{
  "log-driver": "json-file",
  "log-opts": {
    "max-size": "10m",
    "max-file": "3",
    "compress": "true"
  }
}
```

Restart Docker after changing: `sudo systemctl restart docker`

## Log Rotation

Without rotation, container logs grow unbounded and eventually fill the disk. Always configure log rotation:

```bash
# Rotate at 10MB, keep 3 files
docker run -d \
  --log-opt max-size=10m \
  --log-opt max-file=3 \
  myapp

# In docker-compose.yml:
#   services:
#     web:
#       logging:
#         driver: json-file
#         options:
#           max-size: "10m"
#           max-file: "3"
```

### Compose Logging Configuration

```yaml
services:
  web:
    image: myapp:latest
    logging:
      driver: json-file
      options:
        max-size: "10m"
        max-file: "5"
        compress: "true"

  api:
    image: myapi:latest
    logging:
      driver: awslogs
      options:
        awslogs-region: us-east-1
        awslogs-group: /ecs/myapi
        awslogs-stream-prefix: api
```

## Log Parsing and Searching

```bash
# Search for errors
docker logs myapp 2>&1 | grep -i error

# Count error occurrences
docker logs myapp 2>&1 | grep -ci error

# Extract JSON logs
docker logs myapp 2>&1 | jq -r 'select(.level == "error") | .message'

# Parse with timestamps
docker logs -t myapp 2>&1 | grep 'ERROR' | head -20

# Follow and grep in real-time
docker logs -f myapp 2>&1 | grep --line-buffered 'ERROR\|WARN'

# Extract request paths from access logs
docker logs myapp 2>&1 | awk '{print $7}' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn | head -20
```

## Log File Locations

With the default json-file driver, logs are stored at:

```bash
# Find log file path
docker inspect --format '{{.LogPath}}' myapp
# Typically: /var/lib/docker/containers/<container_id>/<container_id>-json.log

# Check log file size
du -sh $(docker inspect --format '{{.LogPath}}' myapp)

# Direct log file access (requires root)
sudo cat /var/lib/docker/containers/<id>/<id>-json.log | jq
```

## Structured Logging Best Practices

- Write logs to stdout/stderr (not files) so Docker captures them
- Use structured JSON format for machine-parseable logs
- Include timestamp, level, message, and correlation ID
- Set appropriate log levels: ERROR for failures, WARN for degradation, INFO for operations, DEBUG for development
- Configure log rotation to prevent disk exhaustion
- Forward logs to a centralized system (ELK, Loki, Datadog) for production
- Use the `none` driver with application-level logging for high-throughput services

sections.title: Basic log commands
sections.body: docker logs shows all captured output. Use -f to follow live output. Use --tail N to show last N lines. Use -t to add timestamps. Separate stdout and stderr with shell redirection. Combine --tail with -f to start following from a specific point.

sections.title: Time-based filtering
sections.body: Use --since for logs after a timestamp or relative duration (30m, 2h, 24h). Use --until for logs before a time. Combine both for a time range. Relative durations are most practical for recent debugging. ISO 8601 timestamps for precise historical queries.

sections.title: Logging drivers
sections.body: The logging driver determines log storage: json-file (default, local JSON), local (optimized local), journald (systemd), syslog (remote), fluentd (aggregator), awslogs (CloudWatch), gcplogs (Cloud Logging), none (discard). Only json-file, local, and journald support docker logs. Others require external log viewers. Set per-container with --log-driver or daemon-wide in daemon.json.

sections.title: Log rotation
sections.body: Without rotation, logs grow unbounded and fill the disk. Configure with --log-opt max-size (e.g. 10m) and --log-opt max-file (e.g. 3). In Compose, use the logging.options key. Set daemon-wide defaults in /etc/docker/daemon.json. The compress option reduces storage for rotated files.

sections.title: Structured logging
sections.body: Write logs to stdout/stderr in JSON format for machine parsing. Include timestamp, level, message, and request/correlation ID. Parse with jq. Forward to centralized systems (ELK, Loki, Datadog) via logging drivers or sidecar containers. Use appropriate log levels for different environments.

related.note: Exec provides interactive access; logs provide output history
Best practices include log rotation and structured logging

scripts.name: View Logs
scripts.description: View Docker container logs with optional filtering by time, line count, and pattern

SINCE.description: Show logs since timestamp or relative (e.g. 30m, 2h, 2026-04-10T10:00:00)

UNTIL.description: Show logs until timestamp or relative

TAIL.description: Number of lines from the end (e.g. 100)

TIMESTAMPS.description: Set to any value to show timestamps

GREP_PATTERN.description: Pattern to filter log lines (case-insensitive grep)

scripts.name: Follow Logs
scripts.description: Stream live container logs with optional pattern filtering
scripts.inline: echo "Following logs for '${CONTAINER}' (Ctrl+C to stop)..." && echo '---' && docker logs -f --tail "${TAIL:-50}" ${TIMESTAMPS:+-t} "$CONTAINER" 2>&1 ${GREP_PATTERN:+| grep -i --line-buffered "$GREP_PATTERN"}

TAIL.description: Number of initial lines to show (default: 50)

TIMESTAMPS.description: Set to any value to show timestamps

scripts.name: Log Statistics
scripts.description: Analyze container logs for error counts, log volume, and common patterns
scripts.inline: echo "=== Log Statistics for '${CONTAINER}' ===" && echo '' && TOTAL=$(docker logs "$CONTAINER" 2>&1 | wc -l) && echo "Total lines: $TOTAL" && echo '' && echo '--- Log Level Counts ---' && docker logs "$CONTAINER" 2>&1 | grep -ioE '(ERROR|WARN|INFO|DEBUG)' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn && echo '' && echo '--- Log File Size ---' && LOG_PATH=$(docker inspect --format '{{.LogPath}}' "$CONTAINER" 2>/dev/null) && if [ -n "$LOG_PATH" ] && [ -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then du -sh "$LOG_PATH"; else echo 'Log file not accessible (non-json-file driver or permission denied)'; fi && echo '' && echo '--- Recent Errors (last 10) ---' && docker logs --tail 1000 "$CONTAINER" 2>&1 | grep -i 'error\|exception\|fatal\|panic' | tail -10

scripts.name: Configure Log Rotation
scripts.description: Display the recommended daemon.json logging configuration for log rotation
scripts.inline: MAX_SIZE="${MAX_SIZE:-10m}" && MAX_FILES="${MAX_FILES:-3}" && echo 'Add to /etc/docker/daemon.json:' && echo '' && echo '{' && echo '  "log-driver": "json-file",' && echo '  "log-opts": {' && echo "    \"max-size\": \"${MAX_SIZE}\"," && echo "    \"max-file\": \"${MAX_FILES}\"," && echo '    "compress": "true"' && echo '  }' && echo '}' && echo '' && echo 'Then restart Docker: sudo systemctl restart docker' && echo '' && echo 'For Compose services, add per-service:' && echo '' && echo '  logging:' && echo '    driver: json-file' && echo '    options:' && echo "      max-size: \"${MAX_SIZE}\"" && echo "      max-file: \"${MAX_FILES}\""

tail.description: Number of lines from the end

since.description: Show logs since timestamp or relative duration

until.description: Show logs until timestamp or relative duration

logFileSize.description: Size of the log file on disk

depends.reason: Docker Engine must be installed and running
Container must be running or have run to produce logs

author: dojo-community
